dominantJob.reasoning: Because there are over 10 distinct panels and numerous secondary actions visible simultaneously, which violates the principle of one clear primary action — compared to ChatGPT's focused text input — this scores 3/10.
violations: Left sidebar panels (Context, Files, Progress, Timing) visible before query
Right sidebar panels (Sources, Work Stream) visible before query
Bottom action buttons (Save report, Open full report, Share link)
Mid-screen action buttons (Go deeper, Show risks, Draft reply, What changed?)

visibleReasoning.reasoning: Because the UI dedicates specific, prominent areas to sources, work streams, and progress stages, which strongly supports transparent AI reasoning — similar to Perplexity's inline citations — this scores 9/10.
trustSignals: WORK STREAM panel on the right
TIMING panel tracking Stages and Sources

speedBehavior.reasoning: Because the layout uses fixed-size containers with 'PENDING' states, which prevents layout shifts during loading — similar to Vercel's skeleton loaders — this scores 8/10.
latencyIssues: The sheer number of panels might cause initial render lag
Waiting for all 4 answer quadrants to fill might feel slower than streaming a single text block

qualityDiscipline.reasoning: Because the UI relies heavily on borders for every single panel, creating visual clutter and potential alignment papercuts — compared to Linear's use of whitespace for structure — this scores 6/10.
papercuts: Excessive use of borders on every panel creates a grid-like, rigid feel
Alignment of 'Idle' badge in Answer header feels floating
Density of pills in the Context section feels cramped

contextCompounding.reasoning: Because the UI explicitly shows context sources (Reports, Me, Files) and persona selectors, which indicates the system learns and adapts — similar to Notion AI's workspace awareness — this scores 8/10.
compoundingSignals: FILES IN PLAY panel for uploaded documents
Persona selectors (Founder, Investor, Banker, CEO, Legal, Student)

chromeCollapse.reasoning: Because the UI uses over 15 distinct bordered containers to separate content, which violates the principle of minimal chrome — compared to ChatGPT's whitespace-driven hierarchy — this scores 2/10.
excessChrome: Borders around every left sidebar panel (Context, Files, Progress, Timing)
Borders around every answer quadrant (What it is, Why it matters, etc.)
Borders around right sidebar panels (Sources, Work Stream)
Borders around bottom action buttons (Save, Open, Share)

components.name: Left Sidebar Panels (Context, Files, Progress, Timing)
components.reasoning: Takes up too much horizontal space and attention before a query is even asked. Should be collapsed or integrated elsewhere.

components.name: Main Answer Area (4 quadrants)
components.reasoning: Forcing every answer into 4 specific boxes might not fit all queries, adds visual noise, and wastes space if a section is brief.

components.name: Chat Input ('Continue the live session...')
components.reasoning: This is the main action but it's pushed down and surrounded by clutter. It should be the focal point of the empty state.

components.name: Right Sidebar Panels (Sources, Work Stream)
components.reasoning: Good for trust, but adds to the 3-column clutter. Could be integrated into the main chat flow or hidden behind a toggle.

components.name: Action Buttons (Save report, Open full report, Share link)
components.reasoning: These actions are premature before a report is actually generated. They clutter the interface and should only appear when relevant.

competitorComparison: NodeBench AI attempts to surface all its reasoning and context simultaneously, unlike ChatGPT which hides complexity behind a simple chat interface. While NodeBench excels at visible trust signals (similar to Perplexity's focus on sources), its heavy reliance on bordered panels creates a cluttered, dashboard-like feel rather than a fluid conversational experience. Linear achieves high density without this clutter by relying on typography and whitespace rather than explicit borders.
topIssues: Excessive Chrome: Over 15 bordered containers create severe visual clutter and compete for attention.
Lack of Dominant Job: The primary action (chat input) is overshadowed by empty sidebars and pre-allocated answer boxes.
Rigid Answer Structure: Forcing answers into 4 specific quadrants ('What it is', 'Why it matters', etc.) may not suit all queries and wastes space.
Premature UI Elements: Showing empty 'Sources' and 'Work Stream' panels before a query is asked adds unnecessary cognitive load.
Action Overload: Too many secondary buttons ('Save report', 'Go deeper', persona pills) are visible simultaneously.
recommendations: Remove borders from the 4 answer quadrants and use typography/spacing to separate the sections to reduce visual noise.
Collapse the left and right sidebars by default, or only reveal them once a query has been submitted and data is available.
Elevate the chat input to the center of the screen for the initial 'empty state' to clarify the primary job.
Hide secondary action buttons ('Save report', 'Share link') until an actual report/answer has been generated.
Integrate 'Sources' and 'Work Stream' directly into the chat flow (like Perplexity) rather than dedicating a permanent right sidebar to them.
